notes: Aberdeen Group EVP/CRO (1988-2005) who oversaw the PC Deals/DCT practice that produced the Aug 2003 weeklies; author of this 2026 retrospective memoir
PC Deals writer who authored many of the Aug 2003 weekly commentaries being reassessed; distinct Kastner-family contributor
Publisher of the PC Deals weekly commentary series under review
Launched Athlon 64 desktop CPU (ClawHammer) on Sept 23 2003, the architectural transition not anticipated in Aug 2003 weeklies
Pentium 4 / Celeron competitor throughout the period; did not ship x86-64 (EM64T) in volume until mid-2004 (Nocona Xeon) and 2005 (desktop Pentium 4 6xx)
Shipped Windows XP Professional x64 Edition on April 25, 2005 — 19 months after Athlon 64 launch; mainstream consumer 64-bit Windows (Vista x64, Windows 7 x64) arrived 2006-2009
Built HP Pavilion A220n and A230n — Athlon XP desktops highlighted as value picks in four Aug 2003 weeklies
T2605 Athlon XP 2600+ desktop was a $600-category winner in the Aug 3 2003 weekly
Circuitcity.com was the retail channel featured in all four Aug 2003 weeklies for the Athlon-based PCs
